name: Spin and Grin
description: The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.1-1.3 wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

name: USMeS -Systems Check (D) 1.8 - 2.2w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS Systems Check
This ride is designed to bring everything online… legs, cadence, breathing, and focus, before stepping into Turn It Up Tuesday immediately following. It’s a chance to settle in, find rhythm, and prepare as a group.
✅ Pace: an average of 1.8–2.2 w/kg
✅ Duration: 55 minutes
Although we aim for the ride to settle around a ~2.0 w/kg overall average, expect slight pushes on climbs, and we’ll always enjoy seeing a sprint when the moment presents itself. The priority is falling back in quickly and keeping the group together.
Ride smooth, respect the leader, and work as a unit.
Systems checked. Ready to roll.
#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted

eventSubgroups.name: USMeS -Systems Check (D) 1.8 - 2.2w/kg (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to the USMES Warm-up Ride.
This is one of two weekly warm-up rides, with the goal of working from this group into the next ride with a higher pace. Use this ride to help you build that solid base for your training to build on!!
These Rides are generally low key and to socialize with our members. The group set up and pace is
specifically lower to support and train for our faster groups.
Pace will start off around 1.5w/kg and could work up to 2.5w/kg on hills. With the average pace being near 2w/kg. There may be times, when traversing a hill/bump, where the pace may go higher, but overall we should be around 2w/kg.
New to our rides or to Zwift? The best place to hang-out in the ride is in the front 1/3 of the Yellow Beacons (Leaders) Peloton or Blob. This keeps you in the draft and at the advertised pace and involved with the group.
The Fence will be used if necessary to control the group.
Please don’t chase the flyers, don’t encourage them. You joined the Group ride, then ride with the group. If you are dead set on Flying, then after the ride starts, select quite the ride and you will stay on the rides route and the Fence won’t zap you! Another option is if you have the energy and legs for it, volunteer SWEEP. Help those that need a wheel back to the blob and get your Interval work in for the day!!

United States Military Endurance Sports (USMES) Zwift riders & runners, is a support group for the USMES National Club. We do not require veteran status to join the Zwift group.
USMES Zwift group Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/usmeszwiftr2
USMES National Team Facebook
Discord Channel
https://discord.com/invite/hExnM7B

name: Bikealicious Sweet Spot
description:
For riders who want more than the standard 60 minute ride but 90 minutes is too much, this 80 minute ride hits the Sweet Spot in between!
We ride banded together, no one gets dropped.  All abilities are welcome. 

Course changes weekly to keep it spicy.

name: Epic Run Crew: Tuesday Trot
description:
You survived Monday! Let’s make Tuesday terrific by joining us for a friendly social run.
This is a 45 minute group run. You are welcome to run at your own pace (AYOP). Chatting is encouraged! Talk about food. Make a funny observation. Whatever. We don’t judge. Just have some fun!
Epic Run Crew is based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. We were founded as an in-person club in 2021 and then joined Zwift in June 2022.
Use the Zwift Companion app to join the Epic Run Crew club, for access to exclusive member-only group runs.
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/epicruncrew2/
Instagram: @epicruncrew

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Ride to Conquer Cancer Training Ride
description:
All Zwifters are welcome to join the Ride to Conquer Cancer Training ride dedicated to conquering cancer.

This is a positive and inclusive ride - use it as a training or recovery ride and get social!

Like the 2-day, 200+ KM event in June every year, this is a ride, not a race. Challenge others in a sprint and then drop back with the group to socialize.
Our cycling community comes together to bring awareness to our need to accelerate cancer research. When we Ride together, we conquer cancer together.

The Ride to Conquer Cancer is Canada's largest athletic fundraiser, raising funds to support world leading cancer research - benefiting cancer patients globally.

Princess Margaret is one of the top 5 cancer research centres in the world. Learn more about Princess Margaret, the Ride to Conquer Cancer or to register / donate, visit us at ride2conquer.ca.ca and join us on Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/conquercancer

eventSubgroups.name: Ride to Conquer Cancer Training Ride (E)

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

description:
Looking for something a little more challenging than our awesome Social Rides? Join us as on our "Kick It Up for Tuesday" Group Ride.

Remember that this is NOT A RACE! Like all group rides in real life, please follow the Ride Leader's (Yellow Beacon) pace and announcements. Riders looking for a challenge are encouraged to ride with the Red Beacon and help sweep the group.

Join us on Discord during the ride https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (but please use Push-To-Talk)
And check us out on Facebook for our other amazing group rides. https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/

name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Early [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Turn It Up Tuesday Early 2.2-2.8
Now that you’re Warmed-Up, Time to Turn Up the Intensity!

Ready to dial up the watts? Turn It Up Tuesday Early is a steady C-paced ride designed to push your endurance, sharpen your legs, and build strength for the week ahead.

Pace: 2.2–2.8 w/kg 
Average: ~2.5 w/kg 
Fence: In use to keep the group together—we’ll drop it for sprints, but regroup quickly

Ride Format:
    • Steady, consistent pacing
    • Sprint segments announced in advance—chase your PRs!
    • Regroup behind the beacon after each effort
    • Friendly, focused vibe to help you stay motivated

Expectations:
 Hold a smooth tempo between sprints
 Respect the fence when active—keep the pack tight
 Regroup promptly after any all-out sections

This is your chance to turn up the effort. Perfect for building fitness. Come ready to ride steady, sprint hard, and regroup fast.

Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/uyE2eUh4yE  and join our Zwift Club and FB by searching “US Military eSports”

 #conqueringlimits   #challengeaccepted

eventSubgroups.name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Early [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg (C)

name: Film My Run 500 with NoblePro
description:
Join Stephen Cousins and NoblePro for a 500 metre climb. Set your treadmill to 12% incline and run 4.2km of distance to reach 500 metres elevation. If your treadmill doesn't go that high, set it as high as it will go and join us anyway!

The run will be progressive starting at a walking pace of 5kph rising to 7kph for the final kilometre. Get on to the balls of your toes and get your calf muscles to do the work. This continuous uphill effort will help build strength and endurance for those hilly trail runs

This event is sponsored by the ever popular Noble Pro treadmills. You can get a discount on a NoblePro SMART treadmill from this link https://zwift.run/noble-pro

You can watch the run on the live stream at youtube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

Join the FMR Run Club for more fun events at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:

More information:

https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 5k at your own pace (AYOP). Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

name: RCC Global Club Ride
description:
WELCOME TO THE RAPHA CYCLING CLUB WORLD WIDE GROUP RIDE. 
 
This is a social group workout; a chance every week for members to get together from around the globe, ride, chat and support each other - it’s run as a group workout session with ‘keep everyone together’ enabled so no matter what your pace you’ll stay in the pack - it’s not a race or competition and open for anyone to join us. 
We host 4 rides every week on a Tuesday to suit different time zones around the world (aimed at evenings), join whichever time works best for you. 
 
WHO IS THE RCC?
 
The Rapha Cycling Club (RCC) is, as the name suggests, Rapha’s Cycling Club. The RCC exists to help connect like minded riders from all over the world, underpinned by Rapha’s products and experiences. We ride together across continents and here united within Zwift. 
 
For more information head to: www.Rapha.cc/rcc
 
RIDE LEADERS
 
The rides are led by our friendly team of member ride leaders from around the world.  The Leader (who has a yellow beacon over their head) will ride at a steady pace as set by the workout session. Prompts will appear on screen set by the workout designer Matt Rowe so you know what to do throughout the ride. On most occasions we will have a support ride leader (who will also have a beacon), the ride leaders are there to help support you and keep the banter going - to join the chat use the Zwift Companion smartphone app. 
 
ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS
 
Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.
 
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Today's workout contains 4 x 6 minute blocks of Zone 3, with some surges thrown in for good measure! The Zone 3 riding should feel nice and comfortable, however the surges may sting a little. The surges in each block are only 20 seconds long, but at 110% of FTP so require a focused increase in effort .At the end of each 6 minute block, there is a 1 minute 'Free Ride' - this is your chance to turn an endurance based session into something special! Come event day or when riding with friends, it's likely you want some more power for that final sprint to the cafe right? Give it 100% in each 60 second Free Ride section to help you become more explosive! Stress points 45: #Social
 
 
WORKOUT DESIGNER
 
Matt Rowe is the founder of Rowe & King Coaching (www.roweandking.com) with 25 years of cycling experience, coaching amateur cyclists all over the world to  achieve their personal goals.
 
HELPFUL HINT
 
We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.
You can adjust the difficulty of the workout mid-ride if needed, by using the up/down arrows on the left hand side, under the workout details.
 
HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#rcc

name: Intermountain Healthcare Tuesday Group Ride
description:
Team Intermountain was originally founded 11 years ago by 5 friends that rode together. The camaraderie they built while riding helped each of them to further develop their fitness and overall cycling ability. They felt like other cycling clubs only existed for the benefit of a few; rather than existing for all its members. Each of the original founders balanced family, career, and cycling. So, they understood the importance of involving those who support the cyclist in these activities.

Today these philosophies continue to guide the club. In the last 11 years, the club has grown to become one of the largest in the state of Utah. Now, we're taking to Zwift and expanding our reach to everyone!

IMH hosts a Tuesday night ride all spring and summer at 6PM leaving from Ogden, Utah and now anyone is welcome to join over the colder months. The groups will be similar to those that exist outside. 

Please join our Discord to chat with fellow riders during the ride. https://discord.gg/Nfx82RmHvm

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

description: High speed ride on Cadex Tri TT bikes and DT Swiss Disc wheel set with draft brought to you by WOW (World Of Watts). We will keep 3.1-3.3 pulls in front and enjoy the fast speed created by the group. Join us and have a great time!! Double draft is on and TT bikes will be drafting!

name: Bikealicious Tuesday Tango
description:
We have two categories to choose from C and E. If you choose C you will be in a non-banded paced ride averaging 2.5-3.2 w/kg. You can also choose category E a rubberband ride where everyone works and stays together (a no drop ride).

Open conversation is a must, but if you're the shy one it's okay.
Dad jokes are encouraged.

name: Booty Thompkins
description:
The SweatFest series will be back next fall.  In it's place we have our "one off" non-series races. If you have a specific course you'd like to see added let us know on our FB page. Link below. 

Day and Time: Every Tuesday 3:30pm & 6:30pm Pacific
We use the standard ZRS category system.

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/132629184105216

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: bet-at-home Midweek Ride
description: "Celebrate" midweek with this group ride. Its ideal for riders who want a solide ride in a bunch. We will ride around 2.5-3.0 W/kg, but will adjust this to the group as necessary. Please stay together as a group. Do not forget, its a steady ride and not a race. If you want to race, then please join our other events. Everyone is welcome, as long as you want.

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Late [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Turn It Up Tuesday Late 2.2-2.8
Late Ride…Time to Turn Up the Intensity!

Ready to dial up the watts after a long day? Turn It Up Tuesday Late is a steady C-paced ride designed to push your endurance, sharpen your legs, and build strength for the week ahead.

Pace: 2.2–2.8 w/kg 
Average: ~2.5 w/kg overall
Fence: In use to keep the group together—we’ll drop it for sprints, but regroup quickly

Ride Format:

    • Smooth, consistent pacing with no big surges between sprints
    • Sprint segments announced in advance—chase your PRs!
    • Regroup behind the beacon after each effort
    • Friendly, focused vibe to help you stay motivated

Expectations:
 Hold a steady tempo between sprints
 Respect the fence when active—keep the pack tight
 Regroup promptly after any all-out sections

This is your chance to turn up the effort. Perfect for building fitness and getting stronger every week.

Come ready to ride steady, sprint hard, and regroup fast.

Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/uyE2eUh4yE  and join our Zwift Club and FB by searching “US Military eSports”

#conqueringlimits    #challengeaccepted

eventSubgroups.name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Late [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg (C)

name: Cycle Nation Before and After Dark Ride
description:
Are you a Zwifter looking for an enjoyable late night or early morning group ride to boost your endurance and aerobic fitness? Wednesday's Cycle Nation Nightcrawler Ride is a little different to our other Nightcrawler Rides. We start at a warmup pace of 1.6w/kg and gradually increase to up to 2.0w/kg over the 60 minutes. This ride is perfect for those aiming to build their stamina and improve their overall fitness. Join us to make the most of your early morning or late night ride.
Connect with Cycle Nation:
• Visit our website: www.cyclenation.co.za
• Join our Facebook Group
• Join our Zwift Team
• Join our Strava Club
• Follow us on Twitter
• Follow us on Instagram

name: Cycle Nation Poznan Endurance Sunrise
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Poznań Sunrise ride. Join us for an fun midweek endurance ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and group around the beacon. Most importantly, this isn’t a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

name: The Zombie 5k
description:
Brought to you from the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) this event is called the Zombie Run as for those in the UK this event is in the middle of the night.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 5k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, Tuesday to Saturday and each day there is a different course.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Endurance Beat Pro
description:
Description:
This workout is designed to build your aerobic endurance while maintaining a steady and manageable effort. It consists of a long warm-up, steady state intervals at endurance pace, and a cool-down. Ideal for riders aiming to improve their long-distance capabilities without overreaching.

Structure:
• Warm-Up:
• 10 minutes starting easy and gradually increasing to 55% of FTP.
• Main Set:
• 40 minutes at 70–80% of FTP (Endurance Zone),
• 2:30/3:00 minutes of easy pedaling (55% FTP) between each 40-minute block.
• Cool-Down:
• 10 minutes gradually decreasing effort to % of FTP.

Key Points:
• Focus on maintaining a smooth, efficient pedal stroke.
• Keep your cadence steady between 85–95 rpm.
• Breathing should be controlled; you should be able to hold a conversation during the main intervals.
• Nutrition and hydration are important for sessions lasting over an hour.
Key Elements
Ideal for building aerobic endurance
Keeps attention high thanks to gentle variations
Great for indoor sessions focused on continuous worklink

name: Turn It Up Tuesdays
description:
Welcome to Turn It Up Tuesdays. Whether you’re a new runner getting into fitness, an experienced runner who wants to compete for a new personal best, or an athlete craving a great workout alongside an awesome crew of fellow athletes, these weekly structured workouts promise up-tempo runs that’ll boost you up and help you achieve your running goals. 

#turnituptuesday #tiut

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt1 BIKE
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: Valhalla Fruitiful Team Chase
description:
Welcome to one of the most fascinating ride formats in the Zwift universe, the Valhalla Fruitiful Team Chase.
This is not a race, not a workout, but a pursuit ride of different groups, each with their own leaders, but in one world on one course.
Each team tries to keep to the advised lead-in and lap times to catch up with the team(s) ahead and/or escape the team(s) behind - like a cat and mouse chase! But it's not a race.
Respect your beacons, blob on yellow and help the reds! The better your team blobs, the faster you are.
There is a category for every rider of every level - so find your best fit of the ranges below and you will challenge yourself to a testing (but enjoyable) workout!
In a perfect world, we would blob up in the final kilometres for an epic mini-race, but this isn't always a perfect world. Help your team to not be left behind and to arrive at the afterparty on time.
To find out more about our rides, please visit our Valhalla Ride and Race Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/

name: Cycle Nation Brussels Sunrise
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Brussels Sunrise. Join us for an easy morning coffee ride aimed at Zwifters looking for an easy paced morning group ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

eventSubgroups.name: Cycle Nation Brussels Sunrise (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Getting ready for Ride Joburg? 🚴‍♂️ Join Cycle Nation and Ride Joburg for our weekly Zwift community rides, created to help you train with friends while keeping things fun and social.

We’ll be hosting at least three group rides each week, with the pace varying from 1.3–2.4 W/kg depending on the day. Whether you’re looking for a relaxed spin or a little extra challenge, there’s a ride for you! Each session is led by a ride leader to keep the group together and the vibe positive—no race pressure, just good training and great company.

Come ride with us, stay consistent, and get ready to roll into Ride Joburg with confidence!

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: 3R Steady State Endurance Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

#3r

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt1 RUN
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R Spark: The Taylor Zwift Fan Club
description:
Welcome to the Taylor Zwift Fan Club, in partnership with 3R. Come and join us for the live version of the popular podcast about all the things that matter and a lot of things that don’t. Podcast hosts Tim and MP will be leading the ride and livestreaming from twitch.tv/TaylorZwiftFC – tune in!
The ride itself is a steady-state session where we split into two groups. Expect a sociable session with lots to keep you entertained. It’s not a race.
3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders in Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: NO LIMIT CC - MIDWEEK ROLL OUT
description:
👥 No Limit Cycling Club is a community-led cycling club built around consistency, confidence, and riding together. Riders of all backgrounds and abilities are welcome.

🌤 This midweek session is designed as a short before-work ride to get the legs moving and start the day right. No drama, no pressure, just ride your ride.

🗺 Routes rotate across Zwift worlds including Watopia, France, and Makuri Islands to keep rides fresh and fun.

🧭 Ride leads manage pacing and communication. Sweepers check in with riders and help regroup when needed.

🔒 We begin with a steady warm up together before settling into a controlled group pace. Riders can push a little if they wish, or sit in and ride comfortably with the group. The focus is smooth pacing, good group riding and positive energy. Fence will be used to help keep the group together and make the ride welcoming for newer Zwift riders.**

🎽 Ride with NLCC to unlock and wear the official No Limit CC Zwift kit.

🔁 Other NLCC rides

Sat 7:00 AM GMT**

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nolimit.bike**

name: Europe Morning Race
description:
Fancy a quick morning 5 km race before work? Join us each Wednesday at 6.30 AM CET (5.30 AM UTC) with 13 laps of the May Field, 5.2 km.

Choose the right category according to your speed/pace:
A: 14.0 kph+ / pace 4:10/km or faster
B: 12.0-13.99 kph / pace: 5:00/km - 4:20/km
C: 10.0-11.99 kph / pace: 5:10/km - 6:00 / km
D: 8 - 9.99 kph / pace: 6:10/km or slower

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt2 RUN
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: Cryo-Gen Punch Bunch (E) - KOM and Sprint Challenge
description:
The Punch Bunch is a structured social ride with a spicy twist.

We roll together at a steady 2.2 w/kg around 4 laps of Innsbruckring, keeping the group smooth and social between efforts.

Each lap follows the same format:

Fence OFF at Legsnapper KOM
Go all-out and test your 1-minute power on the climb

Carry it into the Sprint
Hit the banner full gas

Regroup after the Sprint
We ease up and bring everyone back together before rolling on

Then… we go again.


This is perfect if you want:

A controlled endurance ride with purpose

To build and test your repeatability & punch

A fun, social group that always regroups

D riders can sit steady and enjoy the ride, while stronger riders can light it up on each effort.

Same route. Same format. Every week.
Simple, effective, and a lot of fun.

HEALTH: We would advise any rider who is attempting a Zwift ride for the first time to ensure that their health should always take precedence over completing any ride. If you feel unwell or unable to continue please STOP.

NOTE: Exercise contributes to 25% weight loss; Healthy Eating (Not Diet) contributes to the other 75%. Be fit and Healthy.


CRYOGEN Race STRAVA Club https://www.strava.com/clubs/CRYO-GEN


CRYOGEN Social STRAVA Club https://www.strava.com/clubs/474562


Team CRYO-GEN Race Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/176410539698267

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
Suitable for A riders as a base session, or B & C riders wanting to push themselves.

Examples of the drills are – Z2 with 15sec Z5/6 bursts every 6minutes, alternating Z2/Z3 in various durations, Z2 - Z4 ramps of decreasing duration and many other sessions.

All sessions will be around 60-90mins and will include a warm up so just grab your bottles and head straight to the pen.

This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest surf the draft. This training ride is part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily with each day focusing on a different area of improvement.

Tips: Use the draft to rest and ensure you do not lose the draft.

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together.
The average pace PRIOR to any race finish will be 2.2 wkg. Suitable for D riders looking to become C’s and above
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware!

Please regroup quickly after sprints, intervals or hills and sit between the yellow and red beacons.
This is not a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

name: Rowe & King - Wednesday Wake-up
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Rowe & King's Workout Wednesday is a moderate intensity workout to help build and maintain your aerobic engine. A session suitable for 12 months of the year.


WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Rowe / Rowe & King. Ex Pro Cyclist with experience racing over Europe and European Champion on the track as a Junior in 2005 is now a qualified coach of 11 years. Rowe & King founded in 2016 is a family business with coaches Luke Rowe - Team INEOS Racer, Dani Rowe (formally King) - Olympic Champion London 2016 and Courtney Rowe accompanying Matt along with a team of additional coaches. More info at www.roweandking.com

This is part of the Workout Wednesday live broadcast series with Matt Rowe on Zwift Community Live. 
ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. 

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a nice AYOP (at your own pace) event. Whether you want to push for a new personal best, use it as part of your weekly training program or just enjoy a sociable event this is suitable for you.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020

name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride
description:
This is a social ride welcoming all riders. Our mission is to push you further than before, build your endurance, and have fun. If you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group, if not, just sit in the peloton.

We have the “rubber-band” activated, which means that it’s a no drop ride - no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up, and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. Note: The overall speed may be lower than in other rides due to the banding effect.

So hop on that bike, grab those bottles, and let’s ride!

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Le Col Cycling Club Community Sessions
description:
A structured workout designed by ex-professional cyclist, Chris Opie. The Wednesday Workout Session is all about building your FTP via a mixture of Vo2, Anaerobic and sustainable efforts. These sessions are designed to push you, but also to be achievable and constructive to your fitness, fitting in around longer weekend rides and our other sessions.
Want to know how to get more out of your cycling, nutrition or simply ask our ambassadors about their race days? This is the ride for you!
Train towards challenges, QOMs, KOMs, distance goals, participation goals and build your Zwift XP in good company.
For more information on our training sessions, head here.
https://lecol.cc/pages/events?series=train-with-pros

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1

Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: 4STAR iTT League
description:
4STAR Individual Time Trial League

It’s you versus the clock! The 4STAR Individual Time Trial League is a weekly solo challenge where riders start at set intervals and battle to post their best time. Each week features a different Zwift course — from flat, fast circuits to rolling climbs — giving you a chance to test your pacing, chase down the riders ahead, and smash new personal bests.

Push yourself, measure your progress, and see where you rank among the Zwift community. One rider. One clock. One goal — go faster every week. 🚴‍♂️💨

name: Endure IQ Training Squad Session
description:
--------------------
SESSION FOCUS
--------------------
The aim of this session is to build muscular endurance and durability at threshold. By trageting LT2 we maximally recruit fast oxidative muscle fibers which becoming too glycolytic. The aim is to develop the durability to execute longer threshold workouts.
--------------------
PRE-RIDE:
--------------------
10-Minute Glute Activation (Pre-Ride)
1. Glute Bridge – 2 x 15
2. Fire Hydrants – 10–12 each side
3. Banded Lateral Walks – 10–12 steps each way
4. Single-Leg Deadlift – 8–10 each side
5. Standing Kickbacks – 10–12 each side
Optional Finish: Pogos and 3–5 explosive skips or hops.
----------
Warm up
----------
15min easy build
----------
Main set
----------
15x60sec L4 50-60rpm 60sec easy
----------
Easy to finish
----------

eventSubgroups.name: Endure IQ Training Squad Session (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
--------------------
SESSION FOCUS
--------------------
The focus of this session is to spend as much time as possible at and above ~90% of your VO2max. The priority is on maintaining high power/pace during the work intervals. These sessions are highly stressful but great for generating adaptations that will improve your oxygen-carrying capacity and therefore top-end aerobic capacity. For long-distance triathletes, these sessions can also serve as an effective precursor to longer interval sessions.
Key physiological adaptations we are hunting today:
- Increased maximal cardiac output to push more oxygenated blood each beat
- Greater mitochondrial density so every muscle fiber can process that oxygen
- Improved muscle oxygen extraction to keep power high deep into the set
--------------------
Warm Up
--------------------
15min warm up
--------------------
Main Set
--------------------
5x30sec at FTP +30sec easy high cadence

3min L5 2min easy

2min L5 1min easy

1min L5 40sec easy

40sec L5 30sec easy

2x30sec L5 20sec easy

****3min easy between sets****

Repeat 2-3x

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00421-020-04463-w
--------------------
Cool Down
--------------------

name: PACK Run: Step by Step + After Party
description:
New to running, coming back after some time off, or simply want a nice warm up or cool down session? Join us as we spend 30 minutes moving the legs in this slow-paced, social event. The goal of the event is to encourage folks to move.

The leader will average 8kph (7:30min/km) // 5mph (12min/mile) for the duration and it is expected everyone will stay together. This is a timed event, so we encourage runners to go at their own pace if the average pace is too fast and/or use a run/walk strategy.

**Note: if you utilize a run/walk strategy, please start with the walking portion so that you are not overrunning the group on each interval.**

Put your black and orange tops and socks on and let's get moving!

Facebook Group: #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

Strava Club:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-pack-runners

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

#pack

eventSubgroups.name: PACK Run: Step by Step + After Party (C)

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt3 BIKE
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: INC Relentless Sprint Intervals
description:
The Interval Builder Series uses intervals of varying duration to help you get stronger and faster.  Suitable for C and B riders as an interval session, or A riders as a base session.  These are no sweep rides.

All sessions will be around 60mins and will include a warm up so just grab your bottles and head straight to the pen.

This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest surf the draft. This training ride is part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily with each day focusing on a different area of improvement. 

Tips: Use the draft to rest and ensure you do not lose the draft.

name: True Portfolio Weekly Ride
description:
A weekly group ride for all staff and friends of the True
investment group – which includes the brands Ribble, Frugi, The Cotswold
Company, Zwift, Lo Bros and Nutty Bruce. It’s the taking part that counts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Rapha Women's 100 Training Ride
description:
She’s your greatest competition. Your loudest cheerleader. The one you want to beat. The one you want to win. Rapha and Zwift are celebrating best rivals and fierce friends. The women who give us a wheel to chase. A line to follow. A reason not to hit snooze. Because friendly competition pulls us forward. The harder you go, the further she’ll get.

Join us as we build up to the biggest women's participation event in cycling, the Rapha Women's 100. Returning 5th-6th September 2026.

name: Aussie Crit Crushers Race 1
description:
Aussie Crit Crushers Race is a fun short sharp hitout that consists of two events back to back. When signing up for Race #1 make sure to register for race #2.

Race #1 is on Crit City -Downtown Dolphin ( x4 laps)
Race #2 is on New York -LaGuardia Loop ( x4 laps)

Jump in race both events to be honored winning the XP Cup!
This is a graded Crit race, yes you can crush your mates, whilst also crushing your own PR times each week! 
Com’on what are you waiting for SIGN UP your mates are waiting for you to join them!

name: EVO CC Evolve [2.2 - 2.7w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are designed for Zwifters seeking a diverse, one-hour session that delivers a complete five-zone workout. You can draft within the pack to maintain

Zones 1–3, or push to the front and participate in sprints to hit Zones 4 and 5 during short bursts.

The average pace for this ride is mid-Category C (2.2–2.7 w/kg), though this may fluctuate depending on the course and the leader. Sweepers/keepers will be stationed at the back and will make every effort to assist dropped riders.

Our group thrives on high social interaction, so bring your best banter! Please use the mobile app or a keyboard to chat during the ride.

name: Le rendez-vous du gang
description:
Le mode élastique favorise la cohésion au sein du groupe, permettant à chacun d’exprimer pleinement ses compétences. Ainsi, les défis ne deviennent plus des obstacles, mais plutôt des occasions de partage convivial et de plaisir collectif, transformant l’effort en une expérience enrichissante.

Discutez sur Discord /  Discuss on Discord: https://discord.gg/HgFb6SThT
Notre Facebook: https://m.facebook.com/groups/907351331715445

"Please add “(Gang)” at the end of your username in your Zwift profile.   Merci de mettre (Gang) à la fin de votre pseudo dans votre profil zwift 
"
Ruberband mode encourages group cohesion, allowing everyone to fully express their abilities.      Challenges are no longer obstacles, but opportunities for friendly sharing and collective enjoyment, turning effort into a truly rewarding experience.

name: QueenBee Wellness Wednesday
description:
The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. 



Wellness Wednesday is a social ride aimed at beginners, pre/rehab, those looking for recovery or a low watt ride. Everyone of all abilities are welcome with a social pace around 1.0 - 1.3 wpkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.



Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only):

https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444
